LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ARTHUR LEMPERT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. NEITHER THE 2006 AGREEMENT NOR ANY OTHER ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF IS THE "OWNER" OF THE UNCLAIMED PROPERTY HELD BY DEFENDANT AND IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER IT. THE AGREEMENT IS SILENT AS TO THE ASSETS IN QUESTION AND IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT EVEN LEARN OF THE UNCOLLECTED ASSETS UNTIL 2007 OR 2008. (ORDER ON RULINGS OF STATE CONTROLLER'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, FILED IN OPEN COURT AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT HEARING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT RE: TODAY'S HEARING. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ARTHUR LEMPERT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Plaintiff Arthur Lempert appearing in Pro Per and moving party; Tamar Pachter, Deputy Attorney General, State of California (415) 703-5970 appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Neither the 2006 agreement nor any other admissible evidence establishes that plaintiff is the "owner" of the unclaimed property held by defendant and is entitled to recover it. The agreement is silent as to the assets in question and it is undisputed that plaintiff did not even learn of the uncollected assets until 2007 or 2008. (Order on rulings of State Controller's objections to evidence, filed in open court and copies given to counsel at hearing.) Order signed in open Court re: today's hearing.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Continued To Feb-19-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
